title: C Values, Integer Conversions, and Size Arithmetic · Changed-Lifetime Lab
summary: An independent maintainer ports the checked size and conversion policy to a changed target with tighter memory, different input shape, injected allocation failure, and no worked solution. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of checked size and conversion policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select integer types from range, signedness, rank, width, and representation requirements rather than familiar spelling.
Trace integer promotions and usual arithmetic conversions before comparing, shifting, indexing, or sizing.
Use size_t for object sizes, ptrdiff_t for valid pointer differences, and guarded conversions at API boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Integer Ranges Ranks
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab native-systems team handles memory case 8901. Produce bounded C evidence to reject a size calculation that exceeds its bound; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Integer Ranges Ranks.
